overall_reasoning: Opening the June 1 session with a fully deployed portfolio (essentially $0 cash). Market-wide sentiment is neutral (-0.03) but there are constructive signals: Wall Street is set for record highs per Reuters, AI enthusiasm remains elevated (Nvidia's Huang dismissing disruption fears, software stocks surging), and defense spending headlines remain supportive. Key concerns: a CNBC headline draws parallels to the dotcom bubble top of 2000, and US-Iran tensions linger. My portfolio is well-positioned across tech (NVDA, AAPL, AVGO, CSCO), healthcare (LLY, ABBV), industrials/defense (GE, RTX, LMT), materials (FCX, NEM), consumer discretionary (AMZN), communication (GOOGL, NFLX). Most positions are in the green. NEM is my only position down more than 3% from entry. Since this is the first run of the day and all 1D% changes show 0.00% (pre-market/open data), I should be cautious about acting without confirmed price discovery. I'll hold current positions, review NEM as the one meaningful loser, and assess whether any tactical adjustments make sense. Given the fully invested state, any new buy requires selling something first — and with no clear edge yet at 09:32, pacing my trade budget is prudent.
cash_rationale: Cash is effectively zero (0.01). The portfolio is fully deployed across 14 positions with strong conviction in most names. Given that this is run 1 of 13 today and price discovery has barely begun (all 1D% = 0.00%), I'm treating near-zero cash as appropriate for now — the opportunity set is already captured in current holdings. If intraday momentum reveals a clear new opportunity, I'll need to fund it by trimming an existing position. Maintaining zero cash is defensible given the quality of current holdings, but I'll revisit this as the session develops.

position_reviews.implication: NEM is down -3.07% from entry at $110.04, currently $106.66. I added on 2026-05-29 at near 30-day highs, but gold-related stocks have softened since. The Peter Lynch GARP article today and positive royalty acquisition news are mildly constructive, but the position has moved against me. The thesis (gold holding near highs, NEM as a quality miner) is weakened but not invalidated — gold remains broadly supported. I'll hold for now but will exit if it approaches the -5% threshold (~$104.54) or if gold deteriorates materially.
